impression.title: Impression
impression.subtitle: Auto-generated from findings — editable
impression.placeholder: The impression will appear here as findings are recorded...
impression.autoGenerated: Auto-generated from findings. Edit as needed.

reflux.implausibleConfirm: {value} ms is implausibly high for venous reflux. Confirm this value?

diameter.implausibleConfirm: {value} mm is implausibly large for a vein diameter. Confirm this value?

help.depthMm: Depth from skin to vessel centre — used for endovenous ablation planning
help.compressibility: Complete coaptation under gentle transducer pressure indicates normal patency. Non-compressibility is the primary sign of DVT.
help.thrombosis: Presence and chronicity of intraluminal thrombus (acute = echo-lucent, chronic = echogenic).
help.spontaneity: Spontaneous venous flow at rest — reduced or absent flow suggests obstruction upstream.
help.phasicity: Respiratory variation in flow velocity. Loss of phasicity = proximal obstruction.
help.augmentation: Flow increase with distal compression — absent augmentation suggests obstruction between probe and squeeze site.
help.competency: Auto = derived from findings. Pick a value to override the diagram color.

narrative.sonographerCommentsHelp: Technical notes from the sonographer who performed the study (scanner settings, difficult segments, patient tolerance).
narrative.clinicianCommentsHelp: Interpreting physician supplemental comments — distinct from the auto-generated impression.

normalCompressibilityDeep.right: Normal compressibility of the deep veins in the right lower extremity.
normalCompressibilityDeep.left: Normal compressibility of the deep veins in the left lower extremity.

normalFlowDeep.right: Normal spontaneous phasic augmented flow in the right lower extremity.
normalFlowDeep.left: Normal spontaneous phasic augmented flow in the left lower extremity.

narrative.nonCompressible: Non-compressible segment — {vein}. Finding suggestive of acute DVT.
narrative.partialCompressibility: Partial compressibility — {vein}. Consistent with chronic post-thrombotic changes.
narrative.chronicThrombus: Echogenic, adherent thrombus — {vein}. Consistent with chronic post-thrombotic changes.

gallery.title: Templates
gallery.subtitle: Pick a pre-built case or save your own.
gallery.emptyNoMatch: No templates match "{query}" — try a different search term.
gallery.emptyMine: You haven't saved any templates yet. Fill in a case and click + Save current as template below.
gallery.emptyRecent: Templates you apply will appear here for quick access.

normalBilateral.name: Normal bilateral study
normalBilateral.description: DVT rule-out confirmed normal on both legs.
normalBilateral.impression: Bilateral lower-extremity venous duplex examination is within normal limits. All deep and superficial venous segments are fully compressible with normal spontaneous, phasic, and augmented flow patterns. No evidence of acute or chronic deep vein thrombosis. No pathological reflux identified.

normalRight.name: Normal right only
normalRight.description: Unilateral right-leg study, all segments normal.
normalRight.impression: Right lower-extremity venous duplex examination is within normal limits. Deep and superficial venous segments are fully compressible with normal spontaneous, phasic, and augmented flow patterns. No evidence of acute or chronic deep vein thrombosis. No pathological reflux identified.

normalLeft.name: Normal left only
normalLeft.description: Unilateral left-leg study, all segments normal.
normalLeft.impression: Left lower-extremity venous duplex examination is within normal limits. Deep and superficial venous segments are fully compressible with normal spontaneous, phasic, and augmented flow patterns. No evidence of acute or chronic deep vein thrombosis. No pathological reflux identified.

acuteDvtRightFemoropopliteal.name: Acute DVT — right femoropopliteal
acuteDvtRightFemoropopliteal.description: Occlusive thrombus from right CFV through popliteal.
acuteDvtRightFemoropopliteal.impression: Acute occlusive deep vein thrombosis involving the right common femoral, femoral, and popliteal veins, consistent with femoropopliteal DVT. Segments are non-compressible with absent flow augmentation. Vein calipers are enlarged, supporting acute timing. The distal tibial and peroneal veins are patent with normal compressibility. Superficial venous system appears patent.

acuteDvtLeftFemoropopliteal.name: Acute DVT — left femoropopliteal
acuteDvtLeftFemoropopliteal.description: Occlusive thrombus from left CFV through popliteal.
acuteDvtLeftFemoropopliteal.impression: Acute occlusive deep vein thrombosis involving the left common femoral, femoral, and popliteal veins, consistent with femoropopliteal DVT. Segments are non-compressible with absent flow augmentation. Vein calipers are enlarged, supporting acute timing. The distal tibial and peroneal veins are patent with normal compressibility. Superficial venous system appears patent.

acuteDvtLeftIliofemoral.name: Acute iliofemoral DVT — left
acuteDvtLeftIliofemoral.description: Extensive thrombus from left EIV through mid-FV; May-Thurner workup.
acuteDvtLeftIliofemoral.impression: Acute iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis. Non-compressible segments with absent phasicity and augmentation extending from the external iliac vein through the mid-femoral vein of the left lower extremity. Markedly dilated venous calipers. Distribution raises suspicion for May-Thurner syndrome — recommend cross-sectional imaging of the iliac venous outflow. Distal femoral, popliteal, and calf veins are patent.

acuteDvtRightCalf.name: Isolated calf DVT — right
acuteDvtRightCalf.description: Right PTV + peroneal + gastroc + soleal acute thrombosis.
acuteDvtRightCalf.impression: Acute isolated calf deep vein thrombosis involving the right posterior tibial and peroneal veins, with extension into the gastrocnemius and soleal muscular venous branches. Proximal deep venous system (common femoral, femoral, popliteal) is fully patent. Superficial venous system is patent.

svtGsvRight.name: SVT — GSV right (acute)
svtGsvRight.description: Acute superficial thrombophlebitis of right GSV — SFJ-proximity check.
svtGsvRight.impression: Acute superficial venous thrombophlebitis of the right great saphenous vein, involving the above-knee and proximal calf segments. Non-compressible superficial thrombus extending toward the saphenofemoral junction — document distance from SFJ for management decision. Deep venous system is patent; no evidence of deep extension.

chronicPostThromboticRight.name: Post-thrombotic — right
chronicPostThromboticRight.description: Partial compressibility with chronic changes in right deep system. CEAP C3.
chronicPostThromboticRight.impression: Chronic post-thrombotic changes of the right femoral venous system. Non-dilated, partially compressible segments with echogenic adherent thrombus remnants and reduced spontaneous phasicity. Findings consistent with prior deep vein thrombosis, now chronic. Secondary deep venous insufficiency with combined reflux and obstructive physiology. CEAP C3s Es Ad Pr,o.

chronicGsvRefluxBilateral.name: GSV reflux — bilateral (CEAP C2)
chronicGsvRefluxBilateral.description: Bilateral great saphenous reflux with incompetent SFJ.
chronicGsvRefluxBilateral.impression: Bilateral primary superficial venous reflux involving the great saphenous vein (above-knee and proximal calf segments) and the saphenofemoral junctions. Reflux duration exceeds 1000 ms in all involved segments. Deep venous system is patent with normal compressibility and no evidence of deep reflux. CEAP C2 Ep As Pr — varicose veins of primary etiology, superficial anatomic distribution, reflux pathophysiology.

chronicSsvRefluxRight.name: SSV reflux — right (CEAP C2)
chronicSsvRefluxRight.description: Right small saphenous reflux with incompetent SPJ.
chronicSsvRefluxRight.impression: Primary superficial venous reflux involving the right small saphenous vein and saphenopopliteal junction. Reflux duration exceeds 500 ms in the SSV, consistent with pathological reflux distributed along the posterior calf. Deep venous system is patent with normal compressibility and no evidence of deep reflux. CEAP C2 Ep As Pr.

postAblationGsvRight.name: GSV post-ablation — right
postAblationGsvRight.description: Right GSV segments marked ablated post-EVLA/RFA.
postAblationGsvRight.impression: Status post endovenous ablation of the right great saphenous vein. Ablated GSV segments from above-knee through distal calf demonstrate no flow, consistent with successful occlusion. Saphenofemoral junction stump is patent. Deep venous system is patent with normal compressibility and flow. Small saphenous vein is patent.

recommendations.acuteDvtRightFemoropopliteal: Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation per CHEST 2021 guidelines (DOAC first-line in absence of contraindications). Confirm hospital bridge if applicable. Repeat venous duplex at 3 months to assess resolution and residual thrombus.
recommendations.acuteDvtLeftFemoropopliteal: Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation per CHEST 2021 guidelines (DOAC first-line in absence of contraindications). Confirm hospital bridge if applicable. Repeat venous duplex at 3 months to assess resolution and residual thrombus.
recommendations.acuteDvtLeftIliofemoral: Urgent vascular surgery consultation. Consider catheter-directed thrombolysis or pharmacomechanical thrombectomy for acute iliofemoral DVT per ATTRACT subgroup analysis. Screen for May-Thurner syndrome with CT or MR venography. IVC filter if anticoagulation contraindicated. Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation immediately.
recommendations.acuteDvtRightCalf: Per CHEST 2021 isolated distal (calf) DVT guidance: in a patient with severe symptoms or risk factors for proximal extension, initiate therapeutic anticoagulation for 3 months. Otherwise consider serial duplex surveillance at 1–2 weeks to rule out proximal extension. Evaluate for underlying provocative factors.
recommendations.svtGsvRight: If thrombus extends within 3 cm of the saphenofemoral junction, treat as DVT-equivalent with therapeutic anticoagulation. If > 3 cm from SFJ, prophylactic fondaparinux 2.5 mg daily × 45 days per SURPRISE trial. NSAIDs for symptom relief. Ambulation encouraged. Follow-up duplex in 7–10 days to confirm no deep extension.
recommendations.chronicPostThromboticRight: Graduated elastic compression stockings (30–40 mmHg) daily for post-thrombotic syndrome management. Evaluate for venous ulceration at follow-up. Consider venography or IVUS if stenting of iliac outflow is being considered per ESVS 2022.
recommendations.chronicGsvRefluxBilateral: Endovenous ablation (EVLA or RFA) is indicated for symptomatic GSV reflux per ESVS 2022 (Level Ia evidence). Ambulatory phlebectomy for tributary varicosities. Compression therapy (class II, 20–30 mmHg) as bridge therapy.
recommendations.chronicSsvRefluxRight: Endovenous ablation of the small saphenous vein is appropriate for symptomatic SSV reflux per ESVS 2022. Careful pre-operative mapping of the SPJ and saphenopopliteal anatomy to reduce risk of sural nerve injury. Compression therapy as bridge to definitive treatment.
recommendations.postAblationGsvRight: Continue compression therapy (class II) × 2 weeks post-procedure. Return-to-duplex in 1 month to confirm durable occlusion and exclude endovenous heat-induced thrombosis (EHIT) classes II–IV per Kabnick classification.

sonographerProtocol.dvtRuleOut: Bilateral venous duplex protocol per IAC standards. Compression evaluation every 2 cm from common femoral through tibial and peroneal veins. Colour and spectral Doppler assessment of spontaneity, phasicity, and augmentation. Patient supine with reverse Trendelenburg tilt.
sonographerProtocol.iliofemoralDvt: Iliofemoral DVT protocol: extended proximal imaging through the common femoral into the external and common iliac veins. Documented compressibility, spectral Doppler respiratory phasicity, and caliper measurements from iliac through mid-femoral segments. Contralateral screening for May-Thurner pattern.
sonographerProtocol.calfDvt: Calf-targeted DVT protocol with transverse compression of paired tibial and peroneal veins every 1–2 cm, plus gastrocnemius and soleal muscular branches. Colour Doppler augmentation applied to confirm patency where compression is technically limited.
sonographerProtocol.chronicReflux: Chronic venous insufficiency protocol: reverse Trendelenburg / standing position for reflux. Distal manual augmentation or pneumatic cuff release. Reflux duration measured at SFJ, SPJ, and mid-segments per ESVS 2022 — pathological threshold > 500 ms superficial / > 1000 ms deep.
sonographerProtocol.acuteSvt: Acute superficial venous thrombophlebitis protocol: B-mode measurement of thrombus extent along the great saphenous vein, with specific documentation of the distance from the most proximal thrombus to the saphenofemoral junction. Deep venous system screened in full to rule out extension.
sonographerProtocol.postAblation: Post-endovenous-ablation follow-up protocol. Assessment of ablated GSV segments for occlusion (absent flow, echogenic lumen). SFJ stump inspected for endovenous heat-induced thrombosis (EHIT) per Kabnick classification. Deep venous system screened for thrombus extension.

save.title: Save as template
save.subtitle: Save the current form state as a reusable template for this device.
save.namePlaceholder: e.g. Post-op EHIT II — my lab
save.descriptionPlaceholder: Short description so you can recognize this template later (optional).
save.hint: The template captures findings, CEAP, recommendations, impression, and sonographer comments. No patient data is stored.

delete.confirmBody: This template will be removed from your library. This cannot be undone.

successToast.title: Template deleted
